title: Bermudiana pusilla

This family consists of approximately 150 genera and 600 species. Bermudiana pusilla is further classified in the genus Bermudiana, which is a genus of flowering plants endemic to Bermuda. The genus Bermudiana is part of the family Lythraceae. The specific classification of Bermudiana pusilla as a species is not well-documented in widely available sources.

intro: Bermudiana pusilla, also known as Dwarf Bitter Apple or Bermuda Bitter Apple, is a shrub-like succulent plant native to the Caribbean and tropical America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bermudiana pusilla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
